sections.Goal: Build an automated e-commerce store selling [PRODUCT_TYPE] to [TARGET_AUDIENCE] using [FULFILLMENT_METHOD]. The system handles product sourcing/generation, listing management, order fulfillment, and customer service autonomously.
sections.Why: E-commerce combined with print-on-demand or dropshipping eliminates inventory risk while AI handles product design, listing optimization, and customer service. Profit margins improve as the AI learns which products sell best and optimizes accordingly.
sections.What: An e-commerce storefront with: product catalog with AI-optimized listings, shopping cart and Stripe checkout, automated order routing to fulfillment partners (Printful, suppliers), customer service chatbot, and inventory/margin analytics dashboard.
sections.Context: Tech stack: Next.js 15 (App Router), Stripe for payments, Printful API for print-on-demand fulfillment, Vercel for hosting. Follow Golden Rules — Governance First (human approval for new product lines), Validation Loops (verify fulfillment partner status before routing orders), and Multi-Revenue Streams (cross-sell and upsell automatically).
sections.Blueprint: Phase 1: Storefront with product pages, cart, and checkout. Phase 2: Fulfillment integration — connect to Printful or supplier APIs for automated order routing. Phase 3: AI product generation — create designs and optimize listings based on trends. Phase 4: Customer service automation — order tracking, returns, FAQ chatbot. Phase 5: Analytics — track margins, bestsellers, and customer lifetime value.
sections.Validation: Level 1: TypeScript compiles, ESLint passes. Level 2: Unit tests for cart, checkout, and order routing logic. Level 3: Integration test — add to cart, checkout, verify order sent to fulfillment partner. Level 4: Fulfillment API test — verify order status tracking and webhook handling. Level 5: Customer service chatbot test — verify common queries are handled correctly.

validationGates.name: Syntax & Types
validationGates.description: Ensure code compiles and passes linting

validationGates.name: Unit Tests
validationGates.description: Run all unit tests with coverage reporting

validationGates.name: Order Flow Test
validationGates.description: End-to-end test of the complete order and fulfillment flow

validationGates.name: Fulfillment Integration
validationGates.description: Verify order routing and status tracking with fulfillment partner

commonPitfalls: Not accounting for fulfillment partner downtime — always have fallback routing or queue orders
Ignoring shipping cost calculations — margins can disappear if shipping is not factored into pricing
Missing order status webhooks — customers need real-time tracking updates
Not handling returns and refunds gracefully — automate the return flow to reduce support burden
Over-relying on a single fulfillment partner — diversify to reduce supply chain risk
Skipping product mockup quality — poor product images directly reduce conversion rates
